perturbation.original_text: If at any time, and for any reason other than force majeure affecting the Transmission Line, (i) the Transmission Line is incapable of delivering power at its designed capacity, availability or voltage and curtailment of the Projects of the LIFO Partners would improve the capability of the transmission Line to deliver power at its designed capacity, availability and voltage, or (ii) the Transmission Line line losses exceed 1.14%, the excess line losses have an adverse effect on the Projects of Alpha Mariah, Alpha Mariah (Prime), Beta Mariah, Beta Mariah (Prime), or Gamma Mariah or their Partner Affiliates, and curtailment of the Projects of the LIFO Partners would reduce or eliminate such excess line losses, or (iii) the Transmission Line line losses for either of the power purchase contracts commonly known as Desert Winds I and Desert Winds III exceed the levels experienced immediately prior to the addition of the Projects of the LIFO Partners to the Transmission Line, the excess line losses have an adverse effect on the Projects of Alpha Joshua, Alpha Joshua (Prime), Beta Joshua, Beta Willow or Beta Willow (Prime) or their Partner Affiliates, and curtailment of the Projects of the LIFO Partners would reduce or eliminate such excess line losses, then the Manager shall curtail the Projects of the LIFO Partners.
perturbation.changed_text: If at any time, and for any reason other than force majeure affecting the Transmission Line, (i) the Transmission Line is incapable of delivering power at its designed capacity, availability or voltage and curtailment of the Projects of the LIFO Partners would improve the capability of the transmission Line to deliver power at its designed capacity, availability and voltage, or (ii) the Transmission Line line losses exceed 1.14%, the excess line losses have an adverse effect on the Projects of Alpha Mariah, Alpha Mariah (Prime), Beta Mariah, Beta Mariah (Prime), or Gamma Mariah or their Partner Affiliates, and curtailment of the Projects of the LIFO Partners would reduce or eliminate such excess line losses, then the Manager may curtail the Projects of the LIFO Partners.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'shall' to 'may', the mandatory curtailment of LIFO Partners' projects under specific conditions becomes discretionary for the Manager. This creates an in-text contradiction because the rest of the section assumes that curtailment will happen under these conditions, especially the part that says how the curtailment will happen, which includes an agreement among them concerning the priority of the curtailment of their respective Projects. This change would make the rest of the paragraph unenforceable, because the manager now is not forced to do so, therefore the agreement is unnecessary.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Nothing contained in the preceding sentence shall affect the rights of the Manager to curtail or disconnect the Project of a Partner under Section 4 of that Partner's Technical Use Agreement. Any curtailment under this provision shall be in an amount sufficient to cause the Transmission Line to deliver power at its designed capacity, availability and voltage, or to reduce the Transmission Line line losses, as applicable, up to and including the Projects of all of the LIFO Partners. Such curtailment shall continue until the Manager shall determine that reconnecting the Projects of the LIFO Partners shall not result in the recurrence of the event giving rise to the curtailment. If the LIFO Partners shall inform the Manager in writing of an agreement among them concerning the priority of the curtailment of their respective Projects, the Manager shall effect any curtailment under these provisions according to such priority. If the LIFO Partners fail to so inform the Manager, the Manager shall in its discretion determine which of the Projects of the LIFO Partners to curtail. The Manager shall cooperate with the LIFO Partners to attempt to eliminate the cause of any curtailment under this provision, provided that any expenses in connection with such cooperation shall be paid by the LIFO Partners. In connection with any attempt to eliminate the cause of any curtailment, no change to the Transmission Line shall be made without the prior unanimous consent of the Partners (excluding the LIFO Partners), which consent shall not be unreasonably withheld.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'shall' to 'may' in the Changed Text makes the curtailment of LIFO Partners' projects discretionary for the Manager, contradicting the Contradicted Text. The Contradicted Text outlines a specific procedure for curtailment, including agreements among the LIFO Partners concerning the priority of curtailment. If the Manager is not required to curtail the projects under the conditions specified in the Changed Text, the procedures detailed in the Contradicted Text become unenforceable and create a direct conflict within the document because the assumption that curtailment will happen under specific circumstances is removed, especially because it includes an agreement among them concerning the priority of the curtailment of their respective Projects.

perturbation.original_text: Such curtailment shall continue until the Manager shall determine that reconnecting the Projects of the LIFO Partners shall not result in the recurrence of the event giving rise to the curtailment. If the LIFO Partners shall inform the Manager in writing of an agreement among them concerning the priority of the curtailment of their respective Projects, the Manager shall effect any curtailment under these provisions according to such priority.
perturbation.changed_text: Such curtailment shall continue until the Manager shall determine that reconnecting the Projects of the LIFO Partners shall not result in the recurrence of the event giving rise to the curtailment. If the LIFO Partners shall inform the Manager in writing of an agreement among them concerning the priority of the curtailment of their respective Projects, the Manager will consider such priority.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'shall effect any curtailment under these provisions according to such priority' to 'will consider such priority,' the mandatory adherence to the LIFO Partners' agreed curtailment priority is changed to a discretionary consideration by the Manager. This creates an in-text contradiction, as the previous sentence implies that an agreement between LIFO partners for priority is important, but now the manager may choose to not take that into account, so the agreement becomes pointless. In the rest of the text, it still assumes that the LIFO partners priority will be used.
perturbation.contradicted_text: If the LIFO Partners fail to so inform the Manager, the Manager shall in its discretion determine which of the Projects of the LIFO Partners to curtail. The Manager shall cooperate with the LIFO Partners to attempt to eliminate the cause of any curtailment under this provision, provided that any expenses in connection with such cooperation shall be paid by the LIFO Partners. In connection with any attempt to eliminate the cause of any curtailment, no change to the Transmission Line shall be made without the prior unanimous consent of the Partners (excluding the LIFO Partners), which consent shall not be unreasonably withheld.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated the Manager 'shall effect any curtailment under these provisions according to such priority,' meaning they are obligated to follow the LIFO Partners' agreed priority. The changed text modifies this to the Manager 'will consider such priority,' making it discretionary. The contradicted text states 'If the LIFO Partners fail to so inform the Manager, the Manager shall in its discretion determine which of the Projects of the LIFO Partners to curtail.' This implies that if the LIFO partners DO inform the manager, the manager should follow their instructions, but the change to 'will consider' contradicts this implication. The Manager having discretion only if the LIFO partners fail to agree is contradicted when the manager has discretion even if they do agree, because he will only consider the partner's directions.

perturbation.original_text: In connection with any attempt to eliminate the cause of any curtailment, no change to the Transmission Line shall be made without the prior unanimous consent of the Partners (excluding the LIFO Partners), which consent shall not be unreasonably withheld.
perturbation.changed_text: In connection with any attempt to eliminate the cause of any curtailment, no change to the Transmission Line shall be made without the prior consent of the Manager, which consent shall not be unreasonably withheld.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'unanimous consent of the Partners (excluding the LIFO Partners)' to 'consent of the Manager,' the authority to approve changes to the Transmission Line shifts from the Partners to the Manager. This creates an in-text contradiction, because the original text establishes a partnership agreement, where the partners are allowed to make decisions. The manager can only make decisions regarding curtailment.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Continuing Validity. Except as expressly modified by Section 1 of this Amendment, all of the terms and conditions of the Agreement shall remain in full force and effect. By execution of this Amendment, Alpha Mariah (Prime), Inc. and Beta Mariah (Prime), Inc. hereby become parties to the Agreement and agree to be bound by all of the terms of the Agreement and this Amendment.
perturbation.justification: The original text requires unanimous consent from the Partners (excluding LIFO Partners) for changes to the Transmission Line. The changed text shifts this authority to the Manager. The contradicted text states that all original terms remain in full force and effect, which includes the partner's right to make decisions regarding the transmission line. Therefore, the change creates a contradiction, by taking away a power from the partners, in direct violation of the agreement.
